meta.title: Community Signal — Agentic-AI Watch · Sample
meta.subtitle: A generic worked example of the community-signal dashboard: three categories, ranked share of voice, momentum across two pulls, and evidence-cited filter tuning.

bluf.lead: Runtime platforms own the volume, identity is the fastest riser, and one filter is generating most of the noise.

pillars.title: Identity climbing
pillars.body: Identity overtook the gateway lane for #2.

pillars.title: One noisy filter
pillars.body: A single collision term drives ~40% of rejects.

signals.title: Buyers asking for cross-org agent trust
signals.body: Repeated threads asking how to verify an agent from another company.
signals.why: Direct pull for a verifiable-trust layer.

signals.title: The middle layer is unclaimed
signals.body: No vendor owns cross-organizational agent identity in these communities.
signals.why: Whitespace worth a point of view.

moves.title: Shipped a managed agent runtime
moves.body: Broad launch drove the volume spike this window.

filter_suggestions.rationale: Bare 'AlphaTerm' collides with a game title; scope it to the platform sense.
Uncovered competitor surfacing in identity threads.

items.title: Publish a POV on cross-org agent trust
items.detail: Claim the unclaimed middle before a runtime vendor does.

items.title: Prioritize the verify-an-external-agent flow
items.detail: It maps directly to the demand signal above.

notes: Two Syften pulls (48h) merged and de-duplicated.
Counts are AI-verdict based (accepted + unscored = relevant; rejected = noise).
caveats: Filter suggestions are recommend-only — apply them in the Syften dashboard.
